author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
title: God’s Promise is Sure.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: “But,” say they, “when it is said, ‘If thou believest, thou shalt be saved,’ one of these things is required; the other is offered.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: What is required is in man’s power; what is offered is in God’s.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Why are not both in God’s, as well what He commands as what He offers?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: For He is asked to give what He commands.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Believers ask that their faith may be increased; they ask on behalf of those who do not believe, that faith may be given to them; therefore both in its increase and in its beginnings, faith is the gift of God.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: But it is said thus: “If thou believest, thou shalt be saved,” in the same way that it is said, “If by the Spirit ye shall mortify the deeds of the flesh, ye shall live.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: For in this case also, of these two things one is required, the other is offered.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: It is said, “If by the Spirit ye shall mortify the deeds of the flesh, ye shall live.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Therefore, that we mortify the deeds of the flesh is required, but that we may live is offered.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Is it, then, fitting for us to say, that to mortify the deeds of the flesh is not a gift of God, and not to confess it to be a gift of God, because we hear it required of us, with the offer of life as a reward if we shall do it?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Away with this being approved by the partakers and champions of grace!

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: This is the condemnable error of the Pelagians, whose mouths the apostle immediately stopped when he added, “For as many as are led by the Spirit of God, they are the sons of God;” lest we should believe that we mortify the deeds of the flesh, not by God’s Spirit, but by our own.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: And of this Spirit of God, moreover, he was speaking in that place where he says, “But all these worketh that one and the self-same Spirit, dividing unto every man what is his own, as He will;” and among all these things, as you know, he also named faith.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: As, therefore, although it is the gift of God to mortify the deeds of the flesh, yet it is required of us, and life is set before us as a reward; so also faith is the gift of God, although when it is said, “If thou believest, thou shalt be saved,” faith is required of us, and salvation is proposed to us as a reward.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: For these things are both commanded us, and are shown to be God’s gifts, in order that we may understand both that we do them, and that God makes us to do them, as He most plainly says by the prophet Ezekiel.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: For what is plainer than when He says, “I will cause you to do”?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Give heed to that passage of Scripture, and you will see that God promises that He will make them to do those things which He commands to be done.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: He truly is not silent as to the merits but as to the evil deeds, of those to whom He shows that He is returning good for evil, by the very fact that He causeth them thenceforth to have good works, in causing them to do the divine commands.
